source_report: Video transmitter (VTX) supply chain intelligence for the UAS/drone industry

video_systems.name: DJI FPV System (O3/O4)
video_systems.market_position: Dominant consumer FPV video system. O3/O4 Air Units used in custom builds. Fully proprietary — no interoperability with other systems.
video_systems.strategic_note: DJI FPV system is video-only for custom builds (control via ELRS/Crossfire). DJI's own drones use fully integrated C2+video. ASDA/NDAA bans apply.
video_systems.chip_source: Proprietary DJI SoC (likely HiSilicon or custom Ambarella-derived)

video_systems.name: Walksnail Avatar HD System
video_systems.market_position: Second-largest digital FPV system. Fastest growing. Lower cost than DJI. 400+ distributors in 70+ countries. US/Germany/Australia offices.
video_systems.strategic_note: Walksnail is the primary DJI alternative for digital FPV, but equally Chinese. No NDAA path.

video_systems.name: HDZero / Divimath
video_systems.ndaa_note: Divimath NDAA line: manufactured in Thailand by American/allied-owned factories. HDZero hobby line: assembled in China. Two distinct supply chains.
video_systems.market_position: Third-largest digital FPV. Lowest latency of any digital system. Preferred by racers. Proprietary ASICs (tens of millions USD investment).
video_systems.chip_source: Proprietary Divimath ASICs — designed and owned by Divimath, no off-the-shelf components

ndaa_products.encryption: Encrypted digital video transmission (NDAA line only)
ndaa_products.fc: Divimath Mini FC — STM32H743, ICM42688, manufactured in Thailand, NDAA compliant

video_systems.strategic_significance: CRITICAL — Only NDAA-compliant digital FPV video system. Proprietary ASICs mean no Chinese silicon dependency. Thailand manufacturing avoids China. Divimath is building a complete NDAA drone electronics stack (VTX + FC + goggles).

video_systems.name: Analog 5.8 GHz (various)
video_systems.market_position: Legacy dominant system. Still 37% of VTX in Forge DB. Declining as digital grows. Zero-latency advantage for combat/racing.
video_systems.strategic_note: Analog is the simplest, cheapest, most interoperable video system. Any analog VTX works with any analog VRX. No vendor lock-in. Many analog VTXs use off-the-shelf Realtek or RichWave RF chips (Taiwanese).
video_systems.ndaa_options: ImmersionRC/Orqa (Croatia) and Lumenier (US design) offer analog VTXs with allied supply chains.

video_systems.name: OpenIPC
video_systems.ndaa_note: Uses Chinese SoCs (HiSilicon, Ingenic, Sigmastar). Open-source firmware but Chinese hardware.
video_systems.market_position: Emerging open-source digital FPV. Uses IP cameras repurposed as FPV VTXs. Very low cost. Community-driven.
video_systems.strategic_note: OpenIPC demonstrates that open-source digital FPV is possible, but relies entirely on Chinese SoCs. Not viable for NDAA.

ukrainian_vtx.name: Pilotix
ukrainian_vtx.note: Ukrainian analog VTX manufacturer. Combat-proven in EW-contested environments.

camera_sensor_supply.summary: FPV camera sensors are dominated by Sony (Japan) and OmniVision (US). Both are NDAA-compliant.

vendors.name: Sony Semiconductor Solutions
vendors.note: Premium FPV camera sensors. Used by Caddx, RunCam, Foxeer.

vendors.name: OmniVision (OMNIVISION Technologies)
vendors.ndaa_note: US company but acquired by Chinese consortium (Hua Capital) in 2016. Ownership structure requires verification.
vendors.note: Used in some FPV and machine vision cameras.

camera_sensor_supply.ndaa_status: Sony sensors are clearly NDAA-compliant. OmniVision's Chinese ownership (since 2016) may create compliance questions.
